pi_items.source: WebFetch to target URL confirmed website exists and is a sandbox e-commerce site for scraping practice with ~1000 products and 50 categories

pi_items.verified_value: 561 (Screaming Frog issue threshold) or 600 (Google desktop limit) or 512 (older threshold) or 535 (practical truncation point)
pi_items.source: WebSearch and WebFetch of Screaming Frog official documentation. Current Google SERP thresholds are 600px desktop (practical ~535px), 500px mobile (practical ~475px). Screaming Frog flags 'Over 561 Pixels' issues. No official source mentions 568px.
Filename matches Screaming Frog export conventions for Page Titles tab data
Standard text report filename convention, used in task setup/export scripts
WebSearch confirmed books.toscrape.com has 1000 products across 50 pages + 50 categories = 100+ minimum pages crawlable by Screaming Frog, so 20 rows is reasonable minimum for CSV export

privileged_info_summary: The task targets books.toscrape.com, a verified real sandbox website with ~1000 products for scraping practice. The site contains 50 product pages plus 50 categories, ensuring at least 100+ pages for crawling. The pixel threshold of 568 is inconsistent with official sources: Google currently uses 600px (desktop) with practical truncation at ~535px, mobile uses 500px (~475px practical). Screaming Frog uses 561px as their issue threshold. The expected minimum rows of 20 is reasonable given the site's structure.
